prediction.summary: A low-privilege user, 'www-data', successfully escalated privileges to 'root' on the 'app' server. The attacker then attempted to read the '/etc/shadow' file, indicating an attempt to exfiltrate sensitive credentials. Furthermore, persistence was established by copying '/bin/bash' to '/tmp/.svc_bash' and setting its SUID bit, allowing future execution with root privileges.

timeline.event: A 'chmod' command was executed, flagged as privilege escalation.
Permissions of '/tmp/.svc_bash' changed to SUID (4755) by 'www-data'.
System detected privilege escalation attempt by 'www-data' executing root-level commands.
SIEM generated a CRITICAL alert for privilege escalation abuse (T1548) by 'www-data'.

recommendations: Immediately isolate the compromised host 'app' to prevent further lateral movement or impact.
Investigate the initial vector that allowed the 'www-data' user to execute commands as 'root'. This could indicate a web application vulnerability or misconfiguration.
Remove the malicious SUID binary '/tmp/.svc_bash' and any other persistence mechanisms created by the attacker.
Perform a comprehensive forensic analysis of the compromised system to identify the full scope of the breach, including any data exfiltration or additional backdoors.
Reset all credentials associated with the 'www-data' user and any other potentially compromised accounts. Review all 'root' activity on the host for the past 24-48 hours.
